Meanings: Code to identify geographic location according to U.S. Census. A Tract is an area that sits within a Block.
Identifies the Department. Used to link to other files.
Name of the department
More details about providers in the department. Relates to the encounter.

Overarching, high-level description of department.  "Normal" is outpatient or clinic; HOD (hospital outpatietnd epartment), ED (emergency), OR (Operating Room), LND (labor and delivery), SNF (skilled nursing facility), ICU (intenseive care unit)
Zip code
matches PrimaryDiagnosisKey in Encounters to aid uniquely identifying diagnosis
Info. about patient diagnosis
Code that identifies a specific diagnosis. This should follow a patient throughout all related encounters and can be used to define a patient's "journey"
ICD-10-CM high-level code that identifies the general nature of the diagnosis.
Desription of GroupCode, the high-level description of the diagnosis.
Time and day of admission to hospital or surgery
Where the patient was immediately prior to hospital admission
The reason for hospital admission. If the encounter was not at a hospital, this is "*Not Applicable". Useful for  understanding urgency of visit.
The day of admission as given in AdmissionIntant
The hour of admission
The minute of admission
The month of admission
The year of admission
Identifies the provider for linking to the providers file. But the encounter might be with a robot or machine, in which case the value may be "Unspecified"

date and time of discharge for some visit types.

Year of discharge; from DischargeInstant
unique identifier of row in encounters.

Geographic location of patient's home according to U.S. Census designation.
unique patient identifier; matches with PatientDurableKey in encounters file
patient race. If patient states multiple races, this is the first race given.
Nine marital statuses provided. "*Unspecified" means asked but not answered or the patient was not capable of getting an answer. Unknown means not recorded.

Mychart is an app that allows patients to view their medical records, communicate with care givers, refill prescriptions, consult lab tests, etc. Patients are invited to activate MyChart but may or may not follow through. First, an activation code is generated and, if the patient responds, status changes to "activation pending' and then, finally, "activated".
Ethnicity according to U.S. Office of Management and Budget categories.
Race according to U.S. Office of Management and Budget categories
Birth year of patient in five-year bins. For example, "1970" indicates patient born in (1965, 1970].
Seven categories. "*Unspecified" means patient asked but didn't answer or was not capable of answer. Unknown means nothing was entered.
unspecified means we've addressed the field but haven't answered. Blank means nothing was entered.
last known status
Patient died after 2021 or later.
Licensures of the care provider (i.e. Registered Nurse, Medical Doctor, etc.)

Address of provider's primary work location

The primary location  at which the provider sees  most of their patients.
The provider's specialty. What kind of patients and conditions do they see.

e.g. physician, nurse practitioner, therapist, the field of the provider. A slightly higher level of description than Clinicial Title, which provides more detail. Provides general description of ClinicianTitle.
